title: Flowable BPMN API Schemas
description: JSON Schema definitions extracted from the Flowable BPMN OpenAPI specification

type.description: Can be any arbitrary value. When a valid formatted media-type (e.g. application/xml, text/plain) is included, the binary content HTTP response content-type will be set the given value.

externalUrl.description: contentUrl:In case the attachment is a link to an external resource, the externalUrl contains the URL to the external content. If the attachment content is present in the Flowable engine, the contentUrl will contain an URL where the binary content can be streamed from.

url.description: For a single resource contains the actual URL to use for retrieving the binary resource

mediaType.description: Contains the media-type the resource has. This is resolved using a (pluggable) MediaTypeResolver and contains, by default, a limited number of mime-type mappings.

action.description: Action to perform: Either signal, trigger, signalEventReceived or messageEventReceived

propagatedStageInstanceId.description: The stage plan item instance id this process instance belongs to or null, if it is not part of a case at all or is not a child element of a stage

action.description: Action to perform: Either activate or suspend

includeProcessInstances.description: Whether or not to suspend/activate running process-instances for this process-definition. If omitted, the process-instances are left in the state they are

date.description: Date (ISO-8601) when the suspension/activation should be executed. If omitted, the suspend/activation is effective immediately.

description.example: This is a process for testing purposes

resource.description: Contains the actual deployed BPMN 2.0 xml.

diagramResource.description: Contains a graphical representation of the process, null when no diagram is available.

graphicalNotationDefined.description: Indicates the process definition contains graphical information (BPMN DI).

ProcessInstanceCreateRequest.description: Only one of processDefinitionId, processDefinitionKey or message can be used in the request body

propagatedStageInstanceId.description: The stage plan item instance id this process instance belongs to or null, if it is not part of a case at all or is not a child element of a stage

action.description: Action to perform: Either activate or suspend

variables.description: Array of variables (in the general variables format) to use as payload to pass along with the signal. Cannot be used in case async is set to true, this will result in an error.

tenantId.description: ID of the tenant that the signal event should be processed in

async.description:
If true, handling of the signal will happen asynchronously. Return code will be 202 - Accepted to indicate the request is accepted but not yet executed. If false,
                    handling the signal will be done immediately and result (200 - OK) will only return after this completed successfully. Defaults to false if omitted.

action.description: Action to perform: Either complete, claim, delegate or resolve

assignee.description: If action is claim or delegate, you can use this parameter to set the assignee associated

formDefinitionId.description: Required when completing a task with a form

outcome.description: Optional outcome value when completing a task with a form

variables.description: If action is complete, you can use this parameter to set variables

transientVariables.description: If action is complete, you can use this parameter to set transient variables

delegationState.description: Delegation-state of the task, can be null, "pending" or "resolved".
